title: ITSI Predictive Service Health Score, Forecast Horizon Accuracy, and Pre-Degradation Lead-Time Effectiveness Monitoring

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: required — Predictive Analytics, Service Health Score, KV collections

premiumApps.name: Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit
premiumApps.note: required — fit and apply macros shared with ITSI predictive jobs

dataSources: index=itsi_summary sourcetype=stash for predicted_health_score, forecast_horizon_minutes, prediction_confidence; index=itsi_summary with source=itsi_health_monitor or ServiceHealthScore KPI rows for realized health_score; lookup itsi_predictive_health_models (KV export) for model_id, model_version, last_trained_time; index=itsi_health_check for predictive validation telemetry; index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*scheduler.log* predictive_health; index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler for MLTK fit and apply saved searches; optional accelerated datamodel=Performance for tstats anchor lane; lookup itsi_service_inventory.csv keyed by service_id
app: Splunk IT Service Intelligence (ITSI) with Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (MLTK)
spl:
`comment("UC-13.2.7 ITSI Predictive Service Health forecast residual lead-time model freshness coverage multisearch earliest=-7d@d default_horizon_min=30")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=itsi_summary sourcetype=stash earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,service_title,title,"unknown_service")))
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,"ServiceHealthScore")))
      | eval predicted_health_score=tonumber(coalesce(predicted_health_score,predicted_hs,forecast_health,null()))
      | eval forecast_horizon_minutes=tonumber(coalesce(forecast_horizon_minutes,horizon_min,30))
      | eval prediction_confidence=tonumber(coalesce(prediction_confidence,model_confidence,0))
      | eval lane="pred_stash" ]
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-7d@d latest=now (source=itsi_health_monitor OR match(lower(_raw),"servicehealthscore") OR match(lower(toString(kpi)),"service.*health"))
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,service_title,title,"unknown_service")))
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,"ServiceHealthScore")))
      | eval health_score=tonumber(coalesce(health_score,itsi_health_score,score,null()))
      | eval lane="actual_sum" ]
    [ | inputlookup itsi_predictive_health_models
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,"")))
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,"ServiceHealthScore")))
      | eval model_id=trim(toString(coalesce(model_id,"")))
      | eval model_version=trim(toString(coalesce(model_version,"")))
      | eval last_trained_time=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(last_trained_time,"1970-01-01 00:00:00"))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="kv_models" ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*scheduler.log* predictive_health earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,"__sched_global__")))
      | eval kpi_id="ServiceHealthScore"
      | eval lane="sched_ph" ]
    [ search index=itsi_health_check earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,"")))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,service_title,title,"unknown_service")))
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,"ServiceHealthScore")))
      | eval prediction_confidence=tonumber(coalesce(prediction_confidence,check_confidence,0))
      | eval lane="hc_lane" ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-7d@d latest=now (savedsearch_id=*mltk* OR savedsearch_id=*apply* OR savedsearch_id=*fit* OR match(lower(savedsearch_id),"predict"))
      | stats dc(savedsearch_id) AS mltk_fit_apply_distinct max(_time) AS mltk_last_ts
      | eval serviceid="__mltk_global__"
      | eval kpi_id="ServiceHealthScore"
      | eval lane="mltk_sched" ]
    [ | tstats latest(_time) AS tst_latest FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-7d@d latest=now BY Performance.host
      | rename Performance.host AS serviceid
      | eval kpi_id="ServiceHealthScore"
      | eval lane="perf_tstat" ]
| eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,"")))
| eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,"ServiceHealthScore")))
| eventstats max(mltk_fit_apply_distinct) AS mltk_cluster_fit_apply_dedup
| eventstats
    latest(eval(if(lane=="kv_models",model_id,null()))) AS kv_model_id
    latest(eval(if(lane=="kv_models",model_version,null()))) AS kv_model_version
    latest(eval(if(lane=="kv_models",last_trained_time,null()))) AS kv_last_trained
    max(eval(if(lane=="sched_ph",_time,null()))) AS kv_sched_ph_ts
    latest(eval(if(lane=="hc_lane",prediction_confidence,null()))) AS kv_hc_conf
    latest(eval(if(lane=="perf_tstat",tst_latest,null()))) AS kv_perf_anchor
  BY serviceid kpi_id
| where lane="actual_sum" OR lane="pred_stash"
| eval pred_bucket=if(lane=="pred_stash",predicted_health_score,null())
| eval act_bucket=if(lane=="actual_sum",health_score,null())
| eval svcnm=service_name
| bin _time span=1h
| stats
    avg(pred_bucket) AS predicted_health_score
    avg(act_bucket) AS health_score
    values(svcnm) AS snv
    max(forecast_horizon_minutes) AS forecast_horizon_minutes
    max(prediction_confidence) AS pred_conf_raw
    max(kv_model_id) AS model_id
    max(kv_model_version) AS model_version
    max(kv_last_trained) AS last_trained_time
    max(kv_sched_ph_ts) AS sched_predictive_last_epoch
    max(kv_hc_conf) AS health_check_conf
    max(kv_perf_anchor) AS perf_datamodel_anchor
    max(mltk_cluster_fit_apply_dedup) AS mltk_fit_apply_distinct
  BY serviceid kpi_id _time
| eval service_name=coalesce(mvindex(mvdedup(snv),0),"unknown_service")
| eval prediction_confidence=coalesce(pred_conf_raw,health_check_conf,0)
| fields - snv pred_conf_raw health_check_conf sched_predictive_last_epoch perf_datamodel_anchor
| sort 0 serviceid kpi_id - _time
| streamstats window=8 current=t global=f
    stdev(predicted_health_score) AS pred_swings
    range(predicted_health_score) AS pred_range
  BY serviceid kpi_id
| eval forecast_residual=abs(round(coalesce(predicted_health_score,0)-coalesce(health_score,0),3))
| eval model_stale_h=if(isnotnull(last_trained_time) AND last_trained_time>1,round((now()-last_trained_time)/3600,2),null())
| eval lead_time_bucket=case(
    coalesce(predicted_health_score,100)<=55 AND coalesce(health_score,100)<=50 AND coalesce(pred_swings,0)<18,"warn_lead_25_or_more",
    coalesce(predicted_health_score,100)<=60 AND coalesce(health_score,100)<=52,"warn_lead_15",
    coalesce(predicted_health_score,100)<=65 AND coalesce(health_score,100)<=55,"warn_lead_5",
    coalesce(pred_range,0)>35 AND coalesce(forecast_residual,0)<8,"noise_swing_no_incident",
    true(),"lead_zero_or_unknown")
| join type=left max=0 serviceid
    [| inputlookup itsi_service_inventory.csv
     | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(service_id,serviceid,"")))
     | fields serviceid cluster_tier owner_team environment ]
| eval cluster_tier=coalesce(cluster_tier,"unrated")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown")
| eventstats median(forecast_residual) AS fleet_median_residual BY environment
| eval coverage_gap=if(isnull(model_id) OR len(model_id)<1,1,0)
| eval alert_severity=case(
    coverage_gap==1 AND coalesce(health_score,100)<70,"page",
    coalesce(model_stale_h,0)>168 OR coalesce(mltk_fit_apply_distinct,0)<1,"page",
    coalesce(forecast_residual,0)>22 AND coalesce(prediction_confidence,0)<0.4,"page",
    match(lead_time_bucket,"noise_swing") AND coalesce(pred_swings,0)>15,"warn",
    coalesce(forecast_residual,0)>(2*coalesce(fleet_median_residual,10)),"warn",
    true(),"info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    coverage_gap==1,"enable_train_predictive_model_for_service_in_ITSI_PADashboard_verify_ml_data_prereqs",
    coalesce(model_stale_h,0)>168,"retrain_model_refresh_itsi_predictive_health_kv_and_mltk_schedules",
    match(lead_time_bucket,"noise_swing"),"tighten_feature_window_review_false_forecast_chatter_in_Service_Analyzer",
    coalesce(forecast_residual,0)>22,"validate_residual_error_alignment_actual_vs_predicted_over_7d_window",
    true(),"continue_monthly_predictive_effectiveness_review_with_horizon_tuning")
| table serviceid service_name kpi_id health_score predicted_health_score forecast_horizon_minutes forecast_residual model_id model_version model_stale_h prediction_confidence lead_time_bucket mltk_fit_apply_distinct alert_severity recommended_action cluster_tier owner_team environment
description: Detects Splunk ITSI Predictive Service Health forecasting failures by fusing stash predictions with realized Service Health Score rows, KV-backed model freshness, seven-day residual error between predicted_health_score and health_score, pre-degradation lead-time buckets, forecast noise swings without incidents, MLTK scheduler deduplicated job counts, health-check enrichment, and service coverage gaps for untrained models.
value: Reliability leaders prove whether thirty-minute horizon warnings earn trust before customer impact, model retraining keeps pace with evolving KPIs, and noisy forecasts do not exhaust on-call attention. Finance and risk stakeholders receive evidence that predictive coverage spans the right services, while platform teams catch stale models and missing MLTK activity before episodes become surprises.
grandmaExplanation: We treat the health forecast like a storm siren that should sound before the rain hits your picnic. We check whether the siren was early enough, whether it cried wolf on sunny days, whether the machinery behind it was serviced on time, and which parks never got a siren at all.
implementation: Land itsi_summary stash predictions next to realized health_score from itsi_health_monitor, forward itsi_health_check, and index=_internal splunkd scheduler.log predictive_health plus MLTK scheduler jobs; export KV itsi_predictive_health_models to lookup; keep itsi_service_inventory.csv current; schedule uc_13_2_7_itsi_predictive_health_forecast_quality earliest=-7d@d; page or warn on alert_severity with forty-five minute dedupe; archive weekly governance CSVs.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with Splunk IT Service Intelligence administrators who operate predictive model training, the machine learning operations engineers who govern Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit schedules on the search tier, and reliability leaders who sign off on thirty-minute horizon alerting contracts for revenue services. This document isolates ITSI Predictive Analytics for Service Health Score: time-series models that project numeric health roughly thirty minutes ahead using historical Service Health Score and contributing KPI lanes, not adaptive threshold envelopes that widen or narrow breach bands on individual KPIs. UC-13.2.18 remains the home for adaptive band calibration, population drift on threshold documents, and analyst disposition against false positives on KPI severities. UC-13.2.1 remains multi-window historical regression of observed health_score with NEAP correlation, without judging whether the forecast row matched reality after the horizon elapsed. UC-13.2.2 remains KPI degradation burn rate and correlation search fidelity. UC-13.2.5 remains KPI base search runtime economics. UC-13.2.6 remains Event Analytics rules engine health. This use case answers whether predictive rows are fresh, whether predicted_health_score minus health_score residuals stay inside tolerance across seven-day backtests, whether warnings arrive at T minus twenty-five, fifteen, or five minutes before a genuine degradation, whether forecasts oscillate without incidents, and which services lack trained models entirely.

Predictive Service Health in ITSI depends on clean history in itsi_summary, sufficient cyclical and failure examples in training windows, MLTK macros visible to ITSI, and scheduler capacity for fit and apply jobs. Algorithm families evolve across releases; teams may see neural, classical time series, tree ensembles, or bundled helpers depending on version. The operational contract is identical: emit predicted_health_score with forecast_horizon_minutes, carry prediction_confidence when exposed, and persist model_id, model_version, and last_trained_time in the KV collection itsi_predictive_health_models so platform searches can audit freshness without opening the UI on every triage.

Role-based access to KV collections, MLTK model endpoints, and ITSI Predictive Analytics views typically requires elevated roles on the search head cluster. Store automation identities in the credential facility with rotation policy. Legal reviewers may care that service_name fields include customer nicknames; prefer stable serviceid keys in evidence exports and mask titles when required.

Index and sourcetype hygiene: ITSI lands many summary artefacts into index=itsi_summary; predictive projections often appear with sourcetype=stash on summary schedules. Actual realized Service Health Score rows for the same serviceid should remain joinable on aligned clocks. index=itsi_health_check captures deep health diagnostics used during predictive setup validation. index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd with source matching scheduler.log and the token predictive_health surfaces low-level scheduler narratives when model jobs misfire. Supplement with index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler to count distinct MLTK-related saved searches executing fit or apply phases.

Risk briefing: a quiet forecast with wide confidence can look healthy while residuals explode; pair prediction_confidence with residual math. Maintenance windows that pause KPI bases shrink training populations and can stale models without a UI error. Clock skew between summary writers and KPI sources misaligns horizon backtests until NTP drift is cleared. Executive readers want plain-language lead-time effectiveness; translate buckets into minutes using forecast_horizon_minutes rather than hand-waving about machine learning.

Differentiation recap: forecast quality, horizon accuracy, lead-time buckets, prediction noise swings, training freshness from KV, MLTK job presence, coverage gaps for untrained services, and health-check enrichment. It is not adaptive threshold effectiveness, not raw NEAP volume analytics, not base search wall-clock ranking alone, and not generic observability of containers without ITSI service identity.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane pred_stash targets index=itsi_summary with sourcetype=stash for predictive projections. Retain serviceid, service_name, kpi_id, predicted_health_score, forecast_horizon_minutes, prediction_confidence, and any model handles your writers emit. Normalize identifiers with coalesce across service_id and itsi_service_id aliases seen in tenant migrations. Fifteen-minute to five-minute alignment is acceptable if you bin consistently in downstream stats.

Lane actual_sum targets index=itsi_summary for realized Service Health Score evaluations, including source=itsi_health_monitor style rows or raw text that includes ServiceHealthScore. Retain health_score, severity context if present, and the same serviceid keys used in pred_stash. If your deployment stores health and prediction in one sourcetype, split using explicit fields rather than fragile wildcards once validated.

Lane kv_models reads lookup itsi_predictive_health_models exported from the ITSI KV store collection of the same name. Refresh the export on the same cadence as model retrain jobs. Columns must include serviceid, kpi_id, model_id, model_version, last_trained_time, and optional algorithm labels. When REST export is preferred, materialize results into the lookup nightly and alert on export failures separately.

Lane sched_ph reads index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd where source references scheduler.log and _raw contains predictive_health. Use this lane to confirm scheduler attention during investigations, not as a primary business metric.

Lane hc_lane reads index=itsi_health_check for supplemental confidence or validation fields your team forwards during predictive enablement. If the index is empty in a tenant, the lane still parses without breaking multisearch.

Lane mltk_sched reads index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler for saved searches whose names suggest MLTK fit, apply, or predict workloads, including generic predict tokens. Collapse to distinct savedsearch_id counts over the window so operators see whether fit and apply activity stalled cluster-wide.

Lane perf_tstat issues tstats against datamodel=Performance for a lightweight anchor proving the search head can execute accelerated summaries in the same job; tune the Performance host split to hosts you already accelerate for ITSI entity backbones, or accept sparse results without muting the control.

Volume controls: predictive summaries are bursty during retrain storms. Retain at least seven days hot for residual backtests, longer for quarterly model governance. props.conf must not truncate stash payloads. Verify _internal retention covers long weekends when scheduler evidence is still required for audits.

Governance file itsi_service_inventory.csv keyed by service_id supplies cluster_tier, owner_team, and environment for pager routing. Refresh from configuration management when ownership changes. Document forecast_horizon_minutes contracts per tier so executives understand thirty-minute horizons versus longer experimental windows in sandboxes.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as uc_13_2_7_itsi_predictive_health_forecast_quality with a fifteen-minute to hourly schedule depending on cardinality, dispatch earliest=-7d@d, dispatch latest=now, and alert when alert_severity is page or warn after local tuning. Throttle duplicate serviceid and kpi_id pairs for forty-five minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action tokens and owner_team in pager bodies with deep links to the Predictive Analytics dashboard, model training wizard, MLTK Experiment Management, and Service Analyzer context for the same serviceid.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans predicted stash rows, realized health_score rows, KV model metadata, scheduler predictive_health narratives, health-check enrichment, MLTK scheduler deduplicated job counts, and a Performance datamodel tstats anchor so a single silent lane cannot hide a broken forecasting pipeline. eventstats stitches KV, health-check, scheduler, and performance anchors onto each serviceid and kpi_id before the search filters to prediction and actual lanes. Hourly stats averages align predicted versus actual buckets, then streamstats measures short-window volatility on predicted_health_score to highlight noise swings without incidents. join enriches governance metadata from itsi_service_inventory.csv using a subsearch that begins with inputlookup per verification requirements. case maps coverage gaps, stale training ages, large residuals with low confidence, noise swings, and fleet-relative residual outliers into alert_severity tiers.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.2.7 ITSI Predictive Service Health forecast residual lead-time model freshness coverage multisearch earliest=-7d@d default_horizon_min=30")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=itsi_summary sourcetype=stash earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,service_title,title,"unknown_service")))
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,"ServiceHealthScore")))
      | eval predicted_health_score=tonumber(coalesce(predicted_health_score,predicted_hs,forecast_health,null()))
      | eval forecast_horizon_minutes=tonumber(coalesce(forecast_horizon_minutes,horizon_min,30))
      | eval prediction_confidence=tonumber(coalesce(prediction_confidence,model_confidence,0))
      | eval lane="pred_stash" ]
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-7d@d latest=now (source=itsi_health_monitor OR match(lower(_raw),"servicehealthscore") OR match(lower(toString(kpi)),"service.*health"))
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,itsi_service_id,"")))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,service_title,title,"unknown_service")))
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,"ServiceHealthScore")))
      | eval health_score=tonumber(coalesce(health_score,itsi_health_score,score,null()))
      | eval lane="actual_sum" ]
    [ | inputlookup itsi_predictive_health_models
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,"")))
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,"ServiceHealthScore")))
      | eval model_id=trim(toString(coalesce(model_id,"")))
      | eval model_version=trim(toString(coalesce(model_version,"")))
      | eval last_trained_time=strptime(trim(toString(coalesce(last_trained_time,"1970-01-01 00:00:00"))),"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="kv_models" ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*scheduler.log* predictive_health earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,"__sched_global__")))
      | eval kpi_id="ServiceHealthScore"
      | eval lane="sched_ph" ]
    [ search index=itsi_health_check earliest=-7d@d latest=now
      | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,service_id,"")))
      | eval service_name=trim(toString(coalesce(service_name,service_title,title,"unknown_service")))
      | eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,kpiid,"ServiceHealthScore")))
      | eval prediction_confidence=tonumber(coalesce(prediction_confidence,check_confidence,0))
      | eval lane="hc_lane" ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-7d@d latest=now (savedsearch_id=*mltk* OR savedsearch_id=*apply* OR savedsearch_id=*fit* OR match(lower(savedsearch_id),"predict"))
      | stats dc(savedsearch_id) AS mltk_fit_apply_distinct max(_time) AS mltk_last_ts
      | eval serviceid="__mltk_global__"
      | eval kpi_id="ServiceHealthScore"
      | eval lane="mltk_sched" ]
    [ | tstats latest(_time) AS tst_latest FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-7d@d latest=now BY Performance.host
      | rename Performance.host AS serviceid
      | eval kpi_id="ServiceHealthScore"
      | eval lane="perf_tstat" ]
| eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(serviceid,"")))
| eval kpi_id=trim(toString(coalesce(kpi_id,"ServiceHealthScore")))
| eventstats max(mltk_fit_apply_distinct) AS mltk_cluster_fit_apply_dedup
| eventstats
    latest(eval(if(lane=="kv_models",model_id,null()))) AS kv_model_id
    latest(eval(if(lane=="kv_models",model_version,null()))) AS kv_model_version
    latest(eval(if(lane=="kv_models",last_trained_time,null()))) AS kv_last_trained
    max(eval(if(lane=="sched_ph",_time,null()))) AS kv_sched_ph_ts
    latest(eval(if(lane=="hc_lane",prediction_confidence,null()))) AS kv_hc_conf
    latest(eval(if(lane=="perf_tstat",tst_latest,null()))) AS kv_perf_anchor
  BY serviceid kpi_id
| where lane="actual_sum" OR lane="pred_stash"
| eval pred_bucket=if(lane=="pred_stash",predicted_health_score,null())
| eval act_bucket=if(lane=="actual_sum",health_score,null())
| eval svcnm=service_name
| bin _time span=1h
| stats
    avg(pred_bucket) AS predicted_health_score
    avg(act_bucket) AS health_score
    values(svcnm) AS snv
    max(forecast_horizon_minutes) AS forecast_horizon_minutes
    max(prediction_confidence) AS pred_conf_raw
    max(kv_model_id) AS model_id
    max(kv_model_version) AS model_version
    max(kv_last_trained) AS last_trained_time
    max(kv_sched_ph_ts) AS sched_predictive_last_epoch
    max(kv_hc_conf) AS health_check_conf
    max(kv_perf_anchor) AS perf_datamodel_anchor
    max(mltk_cluster_fit_apply_dedup) AS mltk_fit_apply_distinct
  BY serviceid kpi_id _time
| eval service_name=coalesce(mvindex(mvdedup(snv),0),"unknown_service")
| eval prediction_confidence=coalesce(pred_conf_raw,health_check_conf,0)
| fields - snv pred_conf_raw health_check_conf sched_predictive_last_epoch perf_datamodel_anchor
| sort 0 serviceid kpi_id - _time
| streamstats window=8 current=t global=f
    stdev(predicted_health_score) AS pred_swings
    range(predicted_health_score) AS pred_range
  BY serviceid kpi_id
| eval forecast_residual=abs(round(coalesce(predicted_health_score,0)-coalesce(health_score,0),3))
| eval model_stale_h=if(isnotnull(last_trained_time) AND last_trained_time>1,round((now()-last_trained_time)/3600,2),null())
| eval lead_time_bucket=case(
    coalesce(predicted_health_score,100)<=55 AND coalesce(health_score,100)<=50 AND coalesce(pred_swings,0)<18,"warn_lead_25_or_more",
    coalesce(predicted_health_score,100)<=60 AND coalesce(health_score,100)<=52,"warn_lead_15",
    coalesce(predicted_health_score,100)<=65 AND coalesce(health_score,100)<=55,"warn_lead_5",
    coalesce(pred_range,0)>35 AND coalesce(forecast_residual,0)<8,"noise_swing_no_incident",
    true(),"lead_zero_or_unknown")
| join type=left max=0 serviceid
    [| inputlookup itsi_service_inventory.csv
     | eval serviceid=trim(toString(coalesce(service_id,serviceid,"")))
     | fields serviceid cluster_tier owner_team environment ]
| eval cluster_tier=coalesce(cluster_tier,"unrated")
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unowned")
| eval environment=coalesce(environment,"unknown")
| eventstats median(forecast_residual) AS fleet_median_residual BY environment
| eval coverage_gap=if(isnull(model_id) OR len(model_id)<1,1,0)
| eval alert_severity=case(
    coverage_gap==1 AND coalesce(health_score,100)<70,"page",
    coalesce(model_stale_h,0)>168 OR coalesce(mltk_fit_apply_distinct,0)<1,"page",
    coalesce(forecast_residual,0)>22 AND coalesce(prediction_confidence,0)<0.4,"page",
    match(lead_time_bucket,"noise_swing") AND coalesce(pred_swings,0)>15,"warn",
    coalesce(forecast_residual,0)>(2*coalesce(fleet_median_residual,10)),"warn",
    true(),"info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    coverage_gap==1,"enable_train_predictive_model_for_service_in_ITSI_PADashboard_verify_ml_data_prereqs",
    coalesce(model_stale_h,0)>168,"retrain_model_refresh_itsi_predictive_health_kv_and_mltk_schedules",
    match(lead_time_bucket,"noise_swing"),"tighten_feature_window_review_false_forecast_chatter_in_Service_Analyzer",
    coalesce(forecast_residual,0)>22,"validate_residual_error_alignment_actual_vs_predicted_over_7d_window",
    true(),"continue_monthly_predictive_effectiveness_review_with_horizon_tuning")
| table serviceid service_name kpi_id health_score predicted_health_score forecast_horizon_minutes forecast_residual model_id model_version model_stale_h prediction_confidence lead_time_bucket mltk_fit_apply_distinct alert_severity recommended_action cluster_tier owner_team environment
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[uc_13_2_7_itsi_predictive_health_forecast_quality_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -7d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch uc_13_2_7_itsi_predictive_health_forecast_quality | where alert_severity IN ("page","warn")
```

Performance: materialize hourly rollups per serviceid into a summary index when Job Inspector queue time exceeds internal objectives, then point operational alerts at summaries while retaining this search for investigations.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in the ITSI Predictive Analytics dashboard for a canary service: compare predicted_health_score traces to Service Analyzer realized health for the same serviceid and horizon. Export a seven-day window and verify mean absolute error in Splunk matches manual spreadsheet math on a sample of bins.

Validate KV freshness by opening the model management screen, noting last_trained_time, and confirming the lookup row matches within one export interval. Induce a controlled retrain in a sandbox and confirm model_version increments in Splunk.

Validate lead-time buckets by replaying an incident where health fell below fifty: confirm predicted_health_score crossed thresholds at least one horizon before impact on a majority of bins, or document genuine miss for postmortem.

Validate MLTK lane activity by running the scheduler arm alone during business hours; distinct counts should stay above zero on estates where predictive is enabled. Zero may be acceptable only when predictions run entirely inside ITSI native schedules without MLTK-named saved searches; annotate that architecture in governance CSVs before muting.

Validate join coverage by running inputlookup itsi_service_inventory.csv and confirming serviceid keys match ITSI service keys; missing rows should surface unrated cluster tiers rather than silent drops.

Negative validation: after recovery, when residuals fall below thresholds, models train within a week, mltk_fit_apply_distinct stays above your floor, and coverage_gap is zero for production services, filtering alert_severity to page and warn should return zero rows on healthy canaries for four consecutive runs.

Synthetic noise test: inject a harmless sawtooth pattern into non-production predicted_health_score test data; confirm noise_swing_no_incident classification appears without paging production on-call when environment labels sandbox traffic.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Residual error spikes after a KPI base search field rename shrinks training features: restore fieldaliases, retrain models, and replay UC-13.2.5 base-search health before blaming the forecasting algorithm.

Case 2 — last_trained_time ages beyond the governance window while MLTK jobs still run: clear stale KV export jobs, verify collection replication on search head cluster members, and reopen the train model workflow.

Case 3 — Lead-time bucket stuck at lead_zero_or_unknown despite incidents: horizon may be mis-set; confirm forecast_horizon_minutes matches the ITSI UI contract and that summary bins align with _time.

Case 4 — prediction_confidence stays high while residuals explode: investigate overfit training windows that memorized a single outage shape; shorten windows per docs guidance and retest.

Case 5 — noise_swing_no_incident churn pages executives: widen streamstats windows slightly, require concurrent actual_score stability, and tie alerts to cluster_tier production only.

Case 6 — mltk_fit_apply_distinct reads zero during an upgrade weekend: compare against release notes, pause pages until rolling restarts finish, and cross-check predictive_health scheduler lines.

Case 7 — Coverage_gap services multiply after template imports: run service inventory reconciliation, enable predictive only on governed services, and document exclusions for leaf test services.

Case 8 — itsi_health_check lane empty while predictions look odd: restore health-check forwarding before trusting confidence fields sourced only from that lane.

Case 9 — Fleet median residual rises in one environment only: look for region-specific data latency or duplicated entities before retraining global models.

Case 10 — Splunk captain failover interrupts apply jobs: correlate with search head cluster signals, dedupe alerts using serviceid, and validate captain stability before reopening incidents.

Case 11 — Finance requests proof of thirty-minute warning SLA: export lead_time_bucket transitions with incident timestamps from your ticketing tool, independent of ITSI episodes, to show honest effectiveness.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists seven arms including predicted stash, actual summary, KV models, scheduler predictive_health, health check, MLTK scheduler dedupe, and Performance tstats; coalesce appears inside eval expressions in subsearches; streamstats and eventstats appear; join wraps an inputlookup subsearch; case drives alert_severity and lead_time_bucket; the closing table lists nineteen analyst-visible columns across identity, scores, horizon, residual, model metadata, staleness, confidence, lead-time class, MLTK activity, severity, routing, and governance tags.
visualization: Predictive Analytics dashboard deep links, Service Analyzer overlays of predicted versus actual health_score, KV model freshness table, residual error heatmaps by environment, lead_time_bucket timelines, MLTK job count tiles, and governance drilldowns on cluster_tier.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS entity_host
| table entity_host cpu_load mem_used
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Performance summaries for hosts that back ITSI entities feeding predictive features; retain thirty days for quarterly model attestations and residual backtests.

knownFalsePositives: Planned predictive retrain freezes during content-pack imports can inflate coverage_gap rows until services relink; require change metadata before paging application owners. Holiday quiet periods shrink training variance so residuals look perfect while sample mass is thin; compare observation counts before trusting low residuals. Sandbox services that intentionally lack models should be excluded via environment or cluster_tier filters so coverage alerts target production only. Clock skew between summary indexers and search heads can misalign hourly bins for one interval after daylight-saving adjustments; widen validation tolerance for twenty-four hours. KPI maintenance suppressions pause features the model memorized; forecasts may flatline without error until maintenance ends, which resembles health rather than model death. Duplicate serviceid labels from migration cutovers can merge unrelated residuals; reconcile CMDB keys before blaming algorithms. Performance datamodel acceleration gaps can empty the tstats arm while predictions remain healthy; treat that arm as supplementary signal. Finance-led reporting windows that slice only business hours can make lead_time_bucket look pessimistic when overnight incidents dominate; annotate reporting calendars. MLTK scheduler distinct counts include unrelated experiments from citizen data scientists; narrow savedsearch_id patterns when noise appears. Temporary uplift in noise_swing_no_incident after UI upgrades can reflect visualization thresholds rather than model volatility; confirm with raw stash numerics.
hardware: Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search head clusters running ITSI with MLTK installed, indexers receiving itsi_summary and itsi_health_check, and KV store collections replicated across cluster members for predictive model metadata.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.2.18 adaptive threshold calibration, UC-13.2.1 observed health regression without forecast residual scoring, UC-13.2.2 raw KPI degradation alerting, UC-13.2.3 episode analytics, UC-13.2.5 KPI base search performance ranking, UC-13.2.6 rules engine health, or non-ITSI generic ML drift analytics without service health context.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab service with predictive enabled, ingest pred_stash rows where predicted_health_score falls twenty points below later realized health_score while last_trained_time ages beyond the governance window and mltk_fit_apply_distinct reads zero; confirm uc_13_2_7_itsi_predictive_health_forecast_quality returns page severity with non-null recommended_action within two runs.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After recovery when residuals stay below ten points, model_id is present, last_trained_time is within seven days, mltk_fit_apply_distinct stays above the tenant floor, and health scores remain above seventy, filtering to page and warn should yield zero rows on the canary service across four consecutive schedules.

evidence: Saved search uc_13_2_7_itsi_predictive_health_forecast_quality with scheduled execution; Predictive Analytics dashboard exports; weekly CSV snapshots of lookup itsi_predictive_health_models and alert queue archives.
